rdataretriever v3.0.0
  • Reference
  • Changelog

Download datasets via the Data Retriever.

Source: R/rdataretriever.R
download.Rd

Directly downloads data files with no processing, allowing downloading of non-tabular data.

download(
  dataset,
  path = "./",
  quiet = FALSE,
  sub_dir = "",
  debug = FALSE,
  use_cache = TRUE
)

Arguments

dataset

the name of the dataset that you wish to download
path

the path where the data should be downloaded to
quiet

logical, if true retriever runs in quiet mode
sub_dir

downloaded dataset is stored into a custom subdirectory.
debug

Setting TRUE helps in debugging in case of errors
use_cache

Setting FALSE reinstalls scripts even if they are already installed

Examples

# \donttest{
rdataretriever::download("plant-comp-ok")

#> Error: Python module retriever was not found.
#> 
#> Detected Python configuration:
#> 
#> python:         /usr/bin/python3
#> libpython:      /usr/lib/python3.8/config-3.8-x86_64-linux-gnu/libpython3.8.so
#> pythonhome:     //usr://usr
#> version:        3.8.2 (default, Jul 16 2020, 14:00:26)  [GCC 9.3.0]
#> numpy:          /usr/lib/python3/dist-packages/numpy
#> numpy_version:  1.17.4
#> retriever:      [NOT FOUND]
#> 
# downloaded files will be copied to your working directory
# when no path is specified
dir()

#> [1] "check_for_updates.html"            "check_retriever_availability.html"
#> [3] "commit_log.html"                   "commit.html"                      
#> [5] "data_retriever_version.html"       "dataset_names.html"               
#> [7] "datasets.html"                     "index.html"                       
#> [9] "Rplot001.png"                     
# }
